qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Admin API accessible without authentication on network interfaces
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes missing authentication on the admin API (admin.go:231, admin.go:860-867), which is a different vulnerability from the advisory-described MatchPath case normalization bypass in the HTTP path matcher (modules/caddyhttp/matchers.go). The advisory vulnerability is about percent-encoded paths bypassing case-insensitive path matching, while the finding is about the admin endpoint lacking authentication when bound to non-localhost addresses.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying vulnerability finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a case normalization bypass in Caddy's HTTP path matcher (MatchPath) when percent-escape sequences are used, located in modules/caddyhttp/matchers.go. The discovered finding describes missing authentication on the admin API when configured on non-localhost network interfaces, located in admin.go. These are fundamentally different vulnerabilities in different components with different root causes and attack vectors.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-27587 describes MatchPath case normalization bypass when patterns contain %xx escape sequences
Advisory vulnerable component: modules/caddyhttp/matchers.go in MatchPath.MatchWithError function
Advisory root cause: r.URL.EscapedPath() is not lowercased in the percent-escape matching branch
Advisory impact: path-based routing and access control bypass via case manipulation
Discovered finding vulnerable component: admin.go line 231 and lines 860-867
Discovered finding root cause: enforceHost flag set to false when admin listens on non-localhost
Discovered finding impact: unauthenticated admin API access and server takeover
The two vulnerabilities are in different code paths, different files, and have different security implications
